mnemonics: Don't Drink, Fish, or Eat Tiny Worms
keyPoints: Waterborne diseases: diseases spread through contaminated water, examples include cholera, typhoid, and dysentery.
Water-induced diseases: diseases caused by water pollution, examples include skin diseases like acne and eczema, and eye diseases like conjunctivitis.
Common contaminants in water that cause diseases include bacteria, viruses, and parasites, as well as chemicals and heavy metals.
